heading: Ready Reserve: continuous screening
label: 10149 Ready Reserve: continuous screening

Ex. Ord. No. 11190. Screening of Ready ReserveEx. Ord. No. 11190, , 29 F.R. 19183, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 11382, , 32 F.R. 16247; Ex. Ord. No. 13286, § 67, , 68 F.R. 10630, provided:Dec. 29, 1964Nov. 28, 1967Feb. 28, 2003By virtue of the authority vested in me by of the United States Code, and as President of the United States and Commander in Chief of the Armed Forces of the United States, it is ordered as follows:section 301 of title 31. There is delegated to the Secretary of Defense (and to the Secretary of Homeland Security with regard to the United States Coast Guard) the authority vested in the President by section 271 [see 10149] of title 10 of the United States Code to prescribe regulations for the screening of units and members of the Ready Reserve of the Armed Forces.Section. 2. Executive Order No. 10651 of , is revoked.SecJanuary 6, 1956

Ex. Ord. No. 11190. Screening of Ready ReserveEx. Ord. No. 11190, , 29 F.R. 19183, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 11382, , 32 F.R. 16247; Ex. Ord. No. 13286, § 67, , 68 F.R. 10630, provided:Dec. 29, 1964Nov. 28, 1967Feb. 28, 2003By virtue of the authority vested in me by of the United States Code, and as President of the United States and Commander in Chief of the Armed Forces of the United States, it is ordered as follows:section 301 of title 31. There is delegated to the Secretary of Defense (and to the Secretary of Homeland Security with regard to the United States Coast Guard) the authority vested in the President by section 271 [see 10149] of title 10 of the United States Code to prescribe regulations for the screening of units and members of the Ready Reserve of the Armed Forces.Section. 2. Executive Order No. 10651 of , is revoked.SecJanuary 6, 1956

children.text: That there will be no significant attrition of those members or units during a mobilization.
That there is a proper balance of military skills.
That except for those with military skills for which there is an overriding requirement, members having critical civilian skills are not retained in numbers beyond the need for those skills.
That with due regard to national security and military requirements, recognition will be given to participation in combat.
That members whose mobilization in an emergency would result in an extreme personal or community hardship are not retained in the Ready Reserve.

In applying Ready Reserve continuous screening under this section, an individual who is both a member of the Ready Reserve and a Member of Congress may not be transferred to the Standby Reserve or discharged on account of the individual’s position as a Member of Congress.

children.label: A
children.text: only by the Secretary of Defense or, in the case of a Member of Congress who also is a member of the Coast Guard Reserve, the Secretary of the Department in which the Coast Guard is operating when it is not operating as a service in the Navy; and

children.label: B
children.text: only on the basis of the needs of the service, taking into consideration the position and duties of the individual in the Ready Reserve.
In this subsection, the term “Member of Congress” includes a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to Congress and a Member-elect.
if the member is eligible and applies therefor, transferred to the Retired Reserve.
